scenario: US-Iran fire exchange May 7 (D66): IRGC attacked USS Truxtun/Peralta/Mason east of Hormuz; US sinks 7 Iranian boats; each side claims other fired first. Trump: ceasefire holds ('love tap'). 14-point MOU under review: 12-yr nuclear moratorium, competing blockades lifted over 30-day talks; Iran response via Pakistan within 48hrs (Axios/CNN). Brent $101.26 May 8 (+1.2%, CNBC; off D65 low $101.27); TTF €44.84 (D66 confirmed, carried); JKM $16.84 (D66 confirmed, carried). Hormuz ~90% closed; US blockade active; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; tail risk 35%.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat ticks up slightly (May 8 est.; Brent $101.26 +1.2% on fire exchange, CNBC); Qafco force majeure and Cape rerouting disruption unchanged despite partial de-escalation; fertilizer supply mechanism intact. Fire exchange on May 7 clouds MOU timeline. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation ticks up with Brent $101.26 +1.2% (May 8, CNBC; fire exchange rally); blockade and P&I suspension intact; Cape routing disruption mechanism unchanged; MOU talks ongoing; tail 35%. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting unresolved.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs ticking with Brent $101.26 (May 8, CNBC); fire exchange on May 7 reverts partial de-escalation; Cape routing costs persist; blockade and P&I cancellation intact; structural disruption unresolved; tail 35%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO eases from D63 peak on Brent drop (Brent $101.26 May 8 vs $108.12 D63); fire exchange on May 7 slows further de-escalation; MOU talks ongoing; tail 35%. Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact. Malaysia June supply gap unresolved. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting unresolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure persists; Brent $101.26 May 8 (+1.2%); fire exchange on May 7 clouds MOU timeline; Qafco force majeure and Q2 planting urea disruption unresolved; blockade intact; tail 35%. Malaysia June supply gap risk remains.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha ticks up with Brent $101.26 +1.2% (May 8, CNBC; fire exchange rally); Gulf condensate interdiction unchanged; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; Pengerang RAPID feedstock exposure elevated; MOU talks ongoing; tail 35%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene ticks with naphtha (May 8 est.); SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist despite partial de-escalation; fire exchange on May 7 prevents vessel interdiction from being lifted; blockade intact; MOU review ongoing; tail 35%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eased from D63 peak (May 8 est.; Brent $101.26 +1.2%); US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure unresolved; blockade intact; fire exchange on May 7 - MOU timeline uncertain. China MTO complexes: feedstock pressure moderately elevated. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea ticks up with Brent $101.26 +1.2% (May 8, CNBC; fire exchange rally); Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; MOU talks ongoing; blockade intact. US sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Tail 35%. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP ticks up (May 8 est.; Brent $101.26 +1.2%); Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption unresolved; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade intact; fire exchange on May 7 delays MOU timeline. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam remains acute; tail 35%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight ticks up (May 8 est.; Brent $101.26 +1.2%); less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP; Cape rerouting premium persists; MOU talks may ease freight outlook but fire exchange delays timeline; palm oil plantations facing moderately elevated input costs; tail 35%.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI firms on MOU deal optimism + fire exchange (May 8 est.); Hormuz reopening prospect boosts dry bulk expectations; P&I suspension persists; blockade intact; mine-clearing 6 months even on deal. Fire exchange on May 7 adds uncertainty to normalization timeline. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates eased slightly from D63 peak on Brent drop (May 8 est.; Brent $101.26 vs $108.12 D63); fire exchange on May 7 maintains Hormuz risk premium; MOU deal could unlock AG-Asia fixtures but blockade and P&I suspension unresolved; tail (35%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI carried (weekly; last published Friday May 8). Cape rerouting premium persists - fire exchange on May 7 delays normalization; blockade intact, P&I unresolved. MSC Francesca still seized; Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration + mine-clearing (~6 months); tail 35%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly).
